prompt: Why does MOOSEDev run a single shared backend over a Unix socket instead of letting each MCP client spawn its own server? Answer in 2-3 sentences, and cite the recorded decision's title if you can.

ground_truth.decision_title: Shared backend over Unix-socket MCP for concurrent multi-client access
key_points: RocksDB store is single-writer / takes an exclusive directory lock
per-client stdio servers cannot share one project's store
one shared backend owns the store and serves multiple clients over a Unix socket
